title: Olytic Vault Entry
description: Canonical envelope schema for all vault writes. Every piece of data written to the Olytic vault is wrapped in this envelope regardless of content type. The content field contains the payload — e.g., a session-summary-schema.json object, a document, or analysis results.
read_timing: runtime — read at vault write time, not deploy time
relationship: Envelope wrapper — wraps session-summary-schema.json (or other content payloads) for vault storage

entry_id.description: Unique identifier for this vault entry — generate per write

plugin.description: Kebab-case name of the plugin that created this entry

plugin_version.description: Semver version of the plugin at time of write

session_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this entry was written (typically SessionClose time)

entry_type.description: Type tag that determines vault routing and retention policy

schema_version.description: Version of the content schema used (e.g., '1.0.0' from session-summary-schema.json)

platform.description: The AI platform this vault entry was written from. Hardcoded per plugin at generation time — never inferred at runtime.

content.description: The actual payload — shape depends on entry_type. For session-summary, this is a session-summary-schema.json object.

notes: org_id is injected server-side by the vault gateway — never include it in the entry body
The entry_type tag determines vault partition and retention policy — choose carefully
content is untyped at the envelope level — validation against the content schema is done server-side using schema_version
